A.text: Each pentagon is adjacent only to hexagons.

B.text: No two hexagons share a common edge.

C.text: Two pentagons share a common edge.

D.text: Each hexagon is adjacent only to pentagons.

parsed.answer_value: No two hexagons share a common edge.
parsed.rationale: The image shows a spherical molecular structure composed of interconnected polygons, characteristic of a fullerene. In such structures, the fundamental rule is that no two hexagons can share an edge; they are always separated by at least one pentagon. This arrangement is necessary to close the structure into a sphere. Observing the connections in the provided visualization confirms this pattern, as every hexagonal face is surrounded by pentagons. Therefore, option B accurately describes the observed structural pattern.
